<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Musuhi]][[ja:むすひ]][[pt:Musuhi]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Musuhi]][[ja:むすひ]][[pt:Musuhi]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Musuhi]][[ja:むすひ]][[pt:Musuhi]]

{{Referencesの明記|date=2012nd year 9月}}
'''Musubi''' (むすひ) refers to a concept in {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} describing the spiritual force responsible for the creation, development, and Completion of heaven, earth, and all living things. It is also written as '''産霊''', '''産巣日''', '''産日''', or '''産魂'''.

== Overview ==
The term "musubi" (産) is derived from "umusu" (産むす), with "umu" (to produce) removed, suggesting natural emergence. The "hi" (霊) represents spiritual or mystical energy. In Shinto, all things are believed to arise and develop through the working of "musubi." It is one of the fundamental ideas in Shinto, and its significance has been discussed by {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|qid=Q855159|lt=Kokugaku|1=Kokugaku}} scholars since the Edo period.

Many deities with the word "musubi" in their names are thought to embody this creative force.

Among the {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|lt=Kotoamatsukami|lt=Three Deities of Creation|ja|造化三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Creation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 別天津神}}, there are the deities {{ill|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|1=Takamimusubi}} and {{ill|Kamimusubi|en|Kamimusubi|qid=Q3275901|qid=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi|1=Kamimusubi}}. Takamimusubi played a key role in commanding the deities to bring back {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} when she hid in the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|en|Amano-Iwato|qid=Q3231835|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|1=Amano-Iwato}}, while Kamimusubi revived the slain {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}. These acts demonstrate that the "musubi" deities are associated with invigorating a waning spirit, symbolizing vitality and life force.

Among the {{ill|Eight Imperial Deities|ja|宮中八神|1=Hasshinden|2=ja|3=八神殿|lt=Miyanaka Yatsugami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Imperial Deities|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} enshrined in the {{ill|Imperial Palace|ja|宮中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Palace|1=Kyūchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11453221}}, five include "musubi" in their names: Kamimusubi (神産日神), Takamimusubi (高御産日神), Tamatsume-Musubi (玉積産日神), Ikumusubi (生産日神), and Tarumusubi (足産日神). Tamatsume-Musubi, also known as "Tamodome-Musubi" in the *{{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|qid=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shūi|1=Kogo Shūi}}*, represents the act of anchoring the soul within the body ({{ill|Chinkon|ja|鎮魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon|1=chinkon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10297557}}). Ikumusubi, derived from "iki" (生き, breath), signifies life and vitality. Tarumusubi indicates fullness and abundance in its active force.

The alternate name for {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} is "Homusubi" (火産霊). According to myth, {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} dies after giving birth to Kagutsuchi, the fire god, due to severe burns. Enraged, {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} kills Kagutsuchi, and many gods are born from this act. Kagutsuchi is thus considered a "musubi" deity, embodying the symbolism of life's continuity and renewal, even in death. Similar themes are observed in the story of {{ill|Wakumusubi|en|Wakumusubi|qid=Q10921330|qid=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|1=Wakumusubi}}, who generates grains and plants after death in the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}*.

== List of Deities ==
* {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高御産巣日神|1=Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}
* {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|qid=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}
* {{ill|Tsumusubi-no-Kami|ja|津速産霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumusubi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Amusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|安牟須比命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amusubi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kikitsumusubi-no-Mikoto|ja|己己都牟須比命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikitsumusubi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ikumusubi|ja|天活玉命|1=Ame-no-Ikutama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikumusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Ikutama|qq=}}
* {{ill|Tarumusubi|ja|足産日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumusubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Tamatsume-Musubi|ja|玉積産日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamatsume-Musubi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Wakumusubi|ja|和久産巣日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakumusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワクムスビ}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|ja|火産霊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
